```{r} library(tidyverse) library(tidyselect) library(tibble) library(lubridate) library(sf) library(tigris) library(lubridate) library(tidygeocoder) library(gt) library(gtsummary) # nice count visualizations library(forcats) # a specific data wrangling issue library(readxl) library(wordcloud) library(rstatix) ``` THIS IS STILL A MAJOR WORK IN PROGRESS. I JUST HAVE THE RAW FORMAT HERE. > I need to add Canadian trips to my dataset and exclude them in the United States portion (or maybe incorporate? I don't know at the moment) # The United States ```{r} # Regions defined by the U.S. census bureau northeast <- c("Massachusetts", "New Hampshire", "Vermont", "Connecticut", "Maine", "Rhode Island", "New York", "New Jersey", "Pennsylvania") midwest <- c("Illinois", "Indiana", "Michigan", "Ohio", "Wisconsin", "Iowa", "Kansas", "Minnesota", "Missouri", "Nebraska", "North Dakota", "South Dakota") south <- c("Delaware", "DC", "Florida", "Georgia", "Maryland", "North Carolina", "South Carolina", "Virginia", "West Virginia", "Alabama", "Kentucky", "Mississippi", "Tennessee", "Arkansas", "Louisiana", "Oklahoma", "Texas") west <- c("Arizona", "Colorado", "Idaho", "Montana", "Nevada", "New Mexico", "Utah", "Wyoming", "Alaska", "California", "Hawaii", "Oregon", "Washington") ``` ```{r, output = FALSE} # data that I type in manually daniel_travel_map <- readxl::read_xlsx("trip_data.xlsx") |> # I can also add coordinate information here... maybe play around with that? mutate(start_date = as_date(start_date), end_date=as_date(end_date)) |> mutate(region = case_when( state %in% northeast ~ "Northeast", state %in% midwest ~ "Midwest", state %in% south ~ "South", state %in% west ~ "West" )) |> mutate(travel_length = as.integer(end_date - start_date) + 1) |> mutate(travel_year = year(start_date)) |> drop_na() #any NA is removed # these are places I don't want included us_territories <- c("VI", "GU", "MP", "AS") # US states shapefile us <- states(cb = TRUE, resolution="5m") |> filter(!(STUSPS %in% us_territories)) |> shift_geometry() #for alaska and hawaii (and PR) ``` 1. Let's start out simple: which states have I been to? As a bonus, I will add states I have visited the capital in and states I have lived in (*this is incomplete*). I do not count passing by a state in some form of transportation, but I do count some destination, even if it was unintentional. For example, I originally had a layover in Phoenix, Arizona, but I ended up staying the night. ```{r} # Counts capital_counts <- daniel_travel_map |> filter(capital_indicator == "Yes") |> distinct(state) |> as_vector() visit_counts <- daniel_travel_map |> count(state, name="visits") |> mutate(capital_indicator = ifelse(state %in% capital_counts, 1, 0)) # Also gather information from "lived" dataset ``` ```{r} # Join counts to map travel_count_map <- us |> left_join( visit_counts, by=c("NAME" = "state") ) |> mutate( visits=ifelse(is.na(visits), 0, visits), visit_indicator=ifelse(visits == 0, 0, 1), capital_indicator=ifelse(is.na(capital_indicator), 0, capital_indicator) ) ``` > Currently unconvinced of the dotted and solid lines ```{r, fig.width=18, fig.height=12} # Plot ggplot(data = travel_count_map) + geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(visit_indicator), linetype = factor(capital_indicator)), linewidth = 1) + scale_fill_manual( values = c("0" = "white", "1" = "lightblue"), labels = c("No", "Yes"), name = "Visited State") + scale_linetype_manual( values = c("0" = "dotted", "1" = "solid"), labels = c("No", "Yes"), name = "Visited Capital" ) + theme_bw() + theme( axis.text = element_blank(), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom") + labs( title = "Have I been to the state?" ) ``` 2. Of course I did not visit these states at the same time. This is a step visualization to show when was the first time I landed in a state. This is accurate only from 2018 onwards, since I do not have good data prior to that time. (*Again, I still have yet to add states that I lived in like California and Massachusetts, both of which I first touched in 2008*) ```{r} # first, count unique states from the earliest I've been there first_travel <- daniel_travel_map |> group_by(state) |> filter(start_date == min(start_date)) |> # somehow this worked lol rename(first_visit_date = start_date) |> ungroup(state) first_travel <- first_travel |> mutate(states_visited_order = nrow(first_travel) - row_number() + 1) #again, stupid fix but it works # now we can plot it ggplot(data = first_travel, aes(x = first_visit_date, y = states_visited_order)) + geom_step() + geom_text(aes(label = state, color = region), nudge_x = -225, size = 3) + theme_bw() + labs(x = "Date of First Travel (incomplete)", y = "Number of States", title = "States visited for the first time", color = "Region") ``` 3. As an exercise in small multiple visualizations, I have here a map from each year highlighting how long I stayed in each state I visited each year (by number of days). I also added a border highlight for any state that I lived in. (*Still incomplete with my lived data*) ```{r} # first, loop through the minimum and maximum years of my dataset year_min <- min(daniel_travel_map$travel_year) year_max <- max(daniel_travel_map$travel_year) # I'm going to vectorize this in a minute travel_duration_map <- purrr::map_dfr(year_min:year_max, \(yr) { daniel_travel_map |> filter(travel_year == yr) |> right_join(us, by=c("state" = "NAME")) |> mutate( travel_year = yr, travel_length = coalesce(travel_length, 0) ) }) # some quick selection to make data process cleaner travel_duration_map <- travel_duration_map |> dplyr::select(state, travel_length, travel_year, STUSPS, geometry) |> mutate(lived_indicator = ifelse(state == "Massachusetts", 1, 0)) |> #this is temporary st_as_sf() ``` > I have yet to add my lived data. Maps won't look too different ```{r} # get range of years daniel_lived_data <- readxl::read_xlsx("lived_data.xlsx") # create a new row for each year and each state I have lived in (another loop from 2008 to 2026 and beyond) ``` ```{r, fig.width=18, fig.height=12} # the plot! ggplot(data = travel_duration_map) + geom_sf(aes(fill = travel_length, color = factor(lived_indicator))) + scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Trips") + scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "blue") + scale_color_manual( values = c("0" = "grey40", "1" = "orange"), labels = c("No", "Yes") ) + facet_wrap(~ travel_year) + theme_bw() + theme(axis.text = element_blank(), panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "bottom") + labs(title = "Duration of stay in each state per year", fill = "Length of Travel (Days)", color = "Lived here?") ``` 4. When did I visit each state? This is kind of obselete and not that interesting in my opinion, so I may get rid of it. ```{r} state_dates <- data.frame( state = c( "Delaware","Pennsylvania","New Jersey","Georgia","Connecticut","Massachusetts", "Maryland","South Carolina","New Hampshire","Virginia","New York","North Carolina", "Rhode Island","Vermont","Kentucky","Tennessee","Ohio","Louisiana","Indiana", "Mississippi","Illinois","Alabama","Maine","Missouri","Arkansas","Michigan", "Florida","Texas","Iowa","Wisconsin","California","Minnesota","Oregon", "Kansas","West Virginia","Nevada","Nebraska","Colorado","North Dakota", "South Dakota","Montana","Washington","Idaho","Wyoming","Utah","Oklahoma", "New Mexico","Arizona","Alaska","Hawaii","DC" ), date_joined = as.Date(c( "1787-12-07","1787-12-12","1787-12-18","1788-01-02","1788-01-09","1788-02-06", "1788-04-28","1788-05-23","1788-06-21","1788-06-25","1788-07-26","1789-11-21", "1790-05-29","1791-03-04","1792-06-01","1796-06-01","1803-03-01","1812-04-30", "1816-12-11","1817-12-10","1818-12-03","1819-12-14","1820-03-15","1821-08-10", "1836-06-15","1837-01-26","1845-03-03","1845-12-29","1846-12-28","1848-05-29", "1850-09-09","1858-05-11","1859-02-14","1861-01-29","1863-06-20","1864-10-31", "1867-03-01","1876-08-01","1889-11-02","1889-11-02","1889-11-08","1889-11-11", "1890-07-03","1890-07-10","1896-01-04","1907-11-16","1912-01-06","1912-02-14", "1959-01-03","1959-08-21","1790-07-16" # DC last )) ) daniel_travel_map_ordered <- daniel_travel_map |> mutate(state = factor(state, levels = state_dates$state[order(state_dates$date_joined, decreasing = TRUE)])) ggplot(data = daniel_travel_map_ordered, aes(x = start_date, y = state, color = region)) + geom_point() + theme_bw() + labs(x = "Year", y = "State", color = "Region", title = "Every Visit ordered by when the state joined the union") ``` 5. I tried writing some description of each trip so I could make a wordcloud. Turns out I am not that creative in my writing. ```{r} library(tidytext) library(stopwords) my_stopwords <- stopwords("en", source = "stopwords-iso") travel_wordcloud <- daniel_travel_map |> tidytext::unnest_tokens(word, desc) |> # Breaks text columns into single words count(word, sort=TRUE) |> rename(freq = n) |> #rename for clarity filter(!(word %in% my_stopwords), #remove any stopwords freq > 1) #word should appear more than once # the wordcloud! wordcloud(words = travel_wordcloud$word, freq=travel_wordcloud$freq, min.freq = 150, max.words = 150, random.order=FALSE, rot.per=0.3, colors=brewer.pal(8, "Dark2")) ``` 6. Now for a bunch of descriptive statistics: how many redeyes, how many business vs leisure trips, distribution of days I stayed (faceted by something), average length of trip by days, number of day trips, average distance of trips (MAYBE) etc. I also may change the fourth visualization into a chart instead, with states ordered by when they joined the union. ```{r, results=FALSE} ggplot(data = daniel_travel_map, aes(x = travel_length)) + geom_histogram() + theme_bw() ``` 7. Lines of travel (How have I travelled between each state?): May not work if I don't have proper coordinates. Maybe I can do it based on thickness? *I am unconvinced of this one* 8. A county map? Includes places I've just passed by: something approximate, since the map version here is kind of crazy > I may just do something from mapchart.net ```{r, output = FALSE} us_counties <- counties(cb = TRUE, class="sf") |> filter(!(STUSPS %in% us_territories)) |> shift_geometry() ggplot(data = us_counties) + geom_sf(fill = "white", color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.1) + theme_void() + labs(title = "All US Counties") ```
